parsed_review.summary: Claude Code Opus 4.8 max read-only review reported no P0, no P1, and no material P2 findings. It reported three P3 advisory hardening notes: duplicate ownership boundary array entries are not rejected by the runtime validator, regex policy strings and RegExp constants can drift, and explicit missing-field unit tests could be added. These are non-blocking for this synthetic ownership metadata slice and are deferred to later hardening or validation-helper work.

findings.title: Runtime validator accepts duplicate may_reference or may_not_mutate entries
findings.disposition: Deferred as non-blocking hardening. Contract and fixture evidence are exact-length checked, and this S06 slice only validates synthetic ownership metadata. Future arbitrary instance validation can add uniqueness checks without changing the S06 production_ready result.

findings.title: Policy pattern strings and RegExp constants can drift independently
findings.disposition: Deferred as non-blocking hardening. Current strings and RegExp constants are byte-aligned and validated by tests and the contract script. Deriving one from the other can be handled in a later shared validation-helper slice.

findings.title: Missing-field cases are covered indirectly rather than explicitly
findings.disposition: Deferred as non-blocking test hardening. assertControlPlaneString already fails closed for absent fields, and S06 includes null, array, invalid role, invalid refs, invalid correction route, and boundary omission tests. Explicit missing-key cases can be added in a later hardening pass.
